description: The plan requires `supabase link` and `supabase db push` but never mentions installing the Supabase CLI. The `db:types` script also uses `supabase gen types`.
suggestion: Add to Manual Setup (e.g. as step 0 or in Prerequisites): 'Install Supabase CLI: pnpm add -D supabase, or npm install -g supabase. Required for supabase link, supabase db push, and supabase gen types.'

description: Step 1 tells the user to note the 'direct Postgres connection string' but does not specify where to find it in the Supabase Dashboard.
suggestion: Add: 'Find it in Supabase Dashboard > Project Settings > Database, under Connection string. Use the URI format with port 5432 (direct connection, not transaction pooler).'

description: The file structure shows supabase/migrations/ but does not explain how to create the supabase/ directory. For a new project, supabase init is typically needed.
suggestion: Add to Migration Workflow or Manual Setup: 'For a new project, run supabase init to create the supabase/ directory and config. Then add migration files to supabase/migrations/.'

location: Section 2h, New spot creation with map (line ~279)
description: Two conflicting approaches are proposed: (1) delete the spot on upload failure and return an error, vs (2) allow partial creation and let admin retry map upload later. The plan does not specify which to implement.
suggestion: Choose one approach explicitly. Recommend: 'On failure in step 2 or 3, delete the newly created spot row and return an error to the admin. Do not leave a spot without a map in a partial state.'

location: Section 8 Toast UI vs Section 6/7 publishCourse, enrollInCourse
description: Section 8 states 'Server actions return { success: boolean; error?: string }' for toast handling, but publishCourse returns { course, notificationSent: boolean } and enrollment/other actions may return different shapes.
suggestion: Clarify the return convention: 'For user-facing mutations (enroll, subscribe, etc.), return { success, error? }. For create/update actions (publishCourse), return the entity on success plus optional metadata (e.g. notificationSent); on failure, return { success: false, error } or throw.'

description: The env-config todo lists 'middleware.ts' as a Supabase client setup file. The plan has both src/middleware.ts (Next.js) and src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts (session refresh helper); it is unclear which the todo refers to.
suggestion: Clarify the env-config todo: 'Create ... and Supabase client setup files (client.ts, server.ts in lib/supabase/, plus middleware.ts for session refresh in lib/supabase/).' The auth-flow todo covers creating src/middleware.ts that imports the Supabase helper.

description: The Migration Workflow references Manual Setup steps 6–7 but does not integrate them into a clear sequence. A new implementer may run db commands before completing prerequisites (OAuth, env vars, supabase link).
suggestion: Add a short 'Initial setup sequence' before the run order: '(1) Manual Setup steps 1–5: project, OAuth, Resend, env vars. (2) Run Drizzle generate + migrate. (3) Manual Setup step 6: supabase link. (4) Run supabase db push. (5) Manual Setup step 7: configure Auth Hook in Dashboard. (6) Manual Setup step 8: Vercel when deploying.'

description: The plan describes the success path (token valid → set verified, redirect to /courses?verified=true) but not the failure path when the token is invalid or already used.
suggestion: Add: 'If token is invalid or already used, redirect to /courses?error=invalid_token. Optionally show an error toast on the courses page when this param is present.'

notes: All 8 items applied. MISS-001: Supabase CLI in Prerequisites. MISS-002: Connection string location. MISS-003: supabase init. AMBIG-001: Delete-on-failure for spot creation. INCONSIST-001: Return convention. CLAR-001: env-config todo. STRUCT-001: Initial setup sequence. MISS-004: verify-subscription failure path.
